faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by HiSilicon?
faqs.answer: HiSilicon offers four main product categories: 1) Mobile Processors (Kirin series) - high-performance SoCs for smartphones and tablets with integrated 5G, AI, and multimedia capabilities; 2) AI Accelerators (Ascend series) - dedicated AI chips for edge and data center applications delivering industry-leading TOPS performance; 3) Server Processors (Kunpeng series) - ARM-based server CPUs optimized for cloud computing and enterprise workloads; 4) Connectivity Solutions (Balong series) - 5G modems and connectivity chips for cellular and IoT applications. Each category is designed to deliver best-in-class performance for its target applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find the right HiSilicon solution for your application. Contact our FAE team for product selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right HiSilicon processor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right HiSilicon processor depends on your application requirements: For mobile devices, consider Kirin processors based on performance tier (flagship, mid-range, entry-level) and 5G requirements. For AI applications, evaluate Ascend chips based on required TOPS performance, power budget, and deployment location (edge vs. data center). For server applications, choose Kunpeng CPUs based on core count, memory bandwidth, and workload characteristics. For connectivity, select Balong modems based on cellular standard (5G NR, LTE) and regional band support. BeiLuo's FAE team can provide detailed product comparisons and recommendations based on your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review our product selection guides or contact our FAE team for personalized recommendations based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Kirin, Ascend, and Kunpeng processors?
faqs.answer: Kirin, Ascend, and Kunpeng serve different application domains: Kirin is HiSilicon's mobile processor family designed for smartphones and tablets, featuring integrated CPU, GPU, NPU, ISP, and 5G modem with emphasis on power efficiency and multimedia performance. Ascend is the AI accelerator family optimized for artificial intelligence workloads, offering high TOPS performance for inference and training with specialized AI cores. Kunpeng is the server processor family based on ARM architecture, designed for data center and cloud computing with high core counts, large memory capacity, and enterprise-grade reliability. Each family is optimized for its specific use case and cannot be directly substituted.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your primary application (mobile/AI/server) to determine which processor family is appropriate. Contact us for cross-platform migration guidance if needed.

faqs.question: Does HiSilicon provide software support and development tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, HiSilicon provides comprehensive software support including: 1) Operating system support - Linux, Android, and HarmonyOS; 2) Development toolkits - HiAI SDK for AI development, Kunpeng DevKit for server optimization; 3) Compiler and optimization tools - GCC, LLVM with architecture-specific optimizations; 4) Debugging and profiling tools - performance analyzers, debuggers, and emulators; 5) Reference implementations - sample code, demo applications, and best practices. BeiLuo provides access to these tools along with technical documentation and training resources to accelerate your development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request access to HiSilicon development tools through BeiLuo. Our technical team can assist with tool setup and optimization.

faqs.question: What are the power consumption characteristics of HiSilicon processors?
faqs.answer: HiSilicon processors are designed with power efficiency as a key priority: Kirin mobile processors feature advanced power management with big.LITTLE architecture, achieving 5-10W typical power consumption under load with sub-1W idle states. Ascend AI accelerators offer industry-leading performance-per-watt, with edge devices consuming 5-20W and data center cards ranging 50-300W depending on TOPS requirements. Kunpeng server CPUs deliver competitive performance-per-watt compared to x86 alternatives, with power envelopes from 100W to 200W depending on core count and frequency. All families support advanced power management features including dynamic voltage/frequency scaling and sleep states.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider your power budget and thermal constraints when selecting HiSilicon products. Our FAE team can provide detailed power analysis for your specific use case.

faqs.question: What are the key features of Kirin mobile processors?
faqs.answer: Kirin mobile processors feature: 1) Multi-core CPU architecture with big.LITTLE configuration for optimal performance and efficiency; 2) Integrated Mali GPU delivering high-performance graphics for gaming and multimedia; 3) Dedicated NPU (Neural Processing Unit) for on-device AI acceleration; 4) Advanced ISP supporting multi-camera configurations and computational photography; 5) Integrated 5G modem with support for SA/NSA networks; 6) Advanced security features including hardware encryption and secure boot. These features make Kirin ideal for premium smartphones requiring top-tier performance and AI capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the specifications table to compare Kirin models and identify the best fit for your mobile device requirements.

faqs.question: How do I choose between different Kirin processor models?
faqs.answer: When selecting a Kirin processor, consider: 1) Performance tier - flagship (Kirin 9000 series) for premium devices, mid-range (Kirin 800 series) for mainstream, entry-level (Kirin 700 series) for budget devices; 2) 5G requirements - ensure the model supports your target network bands and carrier requirements; 3) AI capabilities - higher-end models offer more NPU performance for advanced AI features; 4) Display support - verify maximum resolution and refresh rate support; 5) Camera requirements - check ISP capabilities for your camera configuration; 6) Power budget - consider thermal constraints and battery capacity. BeiLuo can provide detailed comparisons and recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your device positioning and key features, then consult our selection guide or contact FAE for model recommendations.

faqs.question: What 5G capabilities do Kirin processors support?
faqs.answer: Kirin processors with integrated Balong modems support comprehensive 5G capabilities: 1) 5G NR SA/NSA dual-mode operation for global network compatibility; 2) Sub-6GHz frequency bands including n1, n3, n28, n41, n77, n78, n79; 3) Carrier aggregation for enhanced data rates; 4) 5G + 4G dual connectivity for seamless handover; 5) mmWave support in select models for ultra-high bandwidth; 6) Power-efficient 5G implementation with intelligent network switching. These capabilities ensure devices can operate on 5G networks worldwide while maintaining excellent battery life through intelligent power management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your target market's 5G band requirements and select a Kirin model with appropriate band support.

faqs.question: What AI capabilities does the Kirin NPU provide?
faqs.answer: The Kirin NPU (Neural Processing Unit) delivers powerful on-device AI acceleration: 1) Dedicated AI cores optimized for neural network inference; 2) Support for popular frameworks including TensorFlow Lite, ONNX, and HiAI; 3) Real-time AI processing for photography (scene recognition, portrait mode, night mode); 4) Natural language processing for voice assistants and translation; 5) Face recognition and biometric authentication; 6) Power-efficient AI operation without waking the main CPU. The latest Kirin 9000 series features a dual-core NPU delivering up to 15 TOPS performance for demanding AI applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate your AI workload requirements and select a Kirin model with appropriate NPU performance for your use cases.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for Kirin processors?
faqs.answer: Kirin processors require appropriate thermal management based on the performance tier: Flagship models (Kirin 9000) typically require vapor chamber or advanced heat pipe cooling for sustained performance under heavy loads; Mid-range models (Kirin 800 series) can use graphite sheets and copper spreaders; Entry-level models have lower thermal requirements. All Kirin processors include advanced thermal management features: dynamic frequency scaling, thermal throttling protection, and intelligent task scheduling. For optimal performance, design your thermal solution to keep junction temperature below 85°C under typical workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your thermal solution based on the expected workload and ambient conditions. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling assistance.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Mobile Applications
faeReview.content: In my experience supporting mobile designs, the Kirin 9000 stands out as one of the most capable flagship processors available. The integrated 5G modem eliminates the power and board space penalties of discrete modem solutions. I particularly recommend this processor for premium smartphones targeting photography enthusiasts - the quad-core ISP combined with the powerful NPU enables computational photography features that rival dedicated camera systems. The thermal design requires attention for sustained gaming workloads, but the intelligent scheduling keeps typical usage comfortable. For AI-heavy applications, the 15 TOPS NPU provides ample headroom for on-device inference without cloud dependency.
faeReview.highlight: Best-in-class integrated 5G solution with exceptional AI and camera capabilities

faqs.question: What is the maximum display resolution supported by Kirin 9000?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 9000 supports up to 4K resolution at 90Hz refresh rate or QHD+ (3200x1440) at 144Hz. The Mali-G78 MP24 GPU provides sufficient performance to drive high-resolution, high-refresh-rate displays smoothly. For optimal power efficiency, the display controller supports adaptive refresh rate technology, dynamically adjusting between 1-144Hz based on content. The display pipeline also supports HDR10+ content with 10-bit color depth for enhanced visual quality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select display panels that match your device's positioning - 144Hz for gaming phones, 90Hz 4K for premium multimedia devices.

faqs.question: How does the integrated 5G modem compare to discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: The integrated Balong 5000 modem in Kirin 9000 offers significant advantages over discrete modem solutions: 1) Lower power consumption - integrated design eliminates inter-chip communication overhead; 2) Reduced board space - no separate modem chip and associated routing; 3) Better thermal management - shared thermal solution; 4) Faster data path - direct connection to CPU and memory; 5) Cost optimization - single chip vs. two chips. The Balong 5000 supports both SA (Standalone) and NSA (Non-Standalone) 5G modes with global band coverage, making it suitable for worldwide deployment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Kirin 9000 for integrated 5G designs requiring optimal power efficiency and compact form factors.

faqs.question: What camera capabilities does the Kirin 9000 ISP support?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 9000 features a quad-core ISP (Image Signal Processor) with advanced camera capabilities: 1) Single camera up to 200MP resolution; 2) Dual camera up to 50MP each with simultaneous processing; 3) 4K video recording at 60fps with HDR; 4) Real-time AI-enhanced photography features including night mode, portrait mode, and scene recognition; 5) Multi-frame noise reduction for low-light performance; 6) Zero-shutter lag for instant capture. The ISP works closely with the NPU to deliver computational photography features previously only possible with dedicated camera systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage the powerful ISP for flagship camera phones targeting photography enthusiasts.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of Kirin 9000?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 9000 power consumption varies by workload: Idle state consumes approximately 0.5-1W; Light usage (web browsing, social media) averages 2-3W; Gaming and intensive applications can reach 5-8W sustained; Peak power during benchmarks may briefly exceed 10W. The 5nm process technology and intelligent power management help maintain excellent battery life despite the high performance. For typical smartphone usage patterns, devices with 4000-4500mAh batteries can achieve full-day usage with the Kirin 9000.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your power and thermal solution for 8W sustained load to ensure consistent performance under demanding conditions.

faqs.question: Does Kirin 9000 support hardware-level security features?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Kirin 9000 includes comprehensive hardware security features: 1) Secure boot with hardware root of trust; 2) Hardware encryption engine supporting AES, RSA, and ECC; 3) Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) for sensitive operations; 4) Hardware-based DRM for content protection; 5) Biometric authentication acceleration for fingerprint and face recognition; 6) Secure key storage in hardware. These features meet enterprise security requirements and support payment applications, digital rights management, and corporate device management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage hardware security features for enterprise devices and applications requiring high security assurance.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for Kirin 9000?
faqs.answer: HiSilicon provides comprehensive development tools for Kirin 9000: 1) HiAI SDK for AI application development with model conversion and optimization tools; 2) Camera SDK for advanced imaging applications; 3) GPU optimization tools for graphics-intensive apps; 4) Performance profiling tools for optimization; 5) Emulation platform for pre-silicon development; 6) Android BSP with device drivers and HAL. BeiLuo provides access to these tools along with technical support, reference designs, and training resources to accelerate your development cycle.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo to access HiSilicon development tools and SDKs for your Kirin 9000 project.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Mobile Systems
faeReview.content: The Kirin 820 is my go-to recommendation for mid-range smartphone designs. It delivers about 80% of flagship performance at roughly half the cost, making it perfect for value-conscious markets. The integrated 5G modem is a game-changer for this segment - competitors often require external modems which add cost and complexity. I've successfully supported several designs with this processor, and the thermal characteristics are very manageable with standard graphite cooling. The 120Hz display support is a nice differentiator in the mid-range segment. For AI features, the 4 TOPS NPU handles most common on-device AI tasks well.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent value proposition for mainstream 5G smartphones with manageable thermal design

faqs.question: What is the performance difference between Kirin 820 and flagship processors?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 820 delivers approximately 70-80% of flagship processor performance in real-world usage. In CPU benchmarks, it scores around 75% of Kirin 9000; GPU performance is about 60% due to fewer cores and lower clock speeds; AI performance at 4 TOPS vs 15 TOPS is sufficient for most on-device AI tasks. For typical smartphone usage including social media, web browsing, and casual gaming, users will find the performance nearly indistinguishable from flagships. The main differences appear in demanding 3D gaming at maximum settings and professional video editing applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Kirin 820 for mainstream devices where cost optimization is important while maintaining good user experience.

faqs.question: Does Kirin 820 support high-refresh-rate displays?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Kirin 820 supports displays up to 120Hz refresh rate at FHD+ resolution (2400x1080). The Mali-G57 MP6 GPU provides sufficient performance to maintain smooth frame rates at 120Hz for UI navigation and most games. The display controller supports adaptive refresh rate technology, dynamically switching between 60Hz and 120Hz based on content to optimize power consumption. This makes the Kirin 820 an excellent choice for devices targeting the popular 120Hz display feature in the mid-range segment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Pair Kirin 820 with 120Hz displays for competitive mid-range smartphones with smooth user experience.

faqs.question: What is the 5G performance of Kirin 820?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 820 integrates a capable 5G modem supporting both SA (Standalone) and NSA (Non-Standalone) modes. It achieves download speeds up to 2.3 Gbps and upload speeds up to 1.2 Gbps under ideal conditions. The modem supports major 5G bands including n1, n3, n28, n41, n77, n78, and n79 for global coverage. In real-world usage, users can expect 100-500 Mbps typical download speeds depending on network conditions. The integrated design provides better power efficiency than external modem solutions, extending battery life during 5G usage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Kirin 820 provides excellent 5G connectivity for mainstream devices with good power efficiency.

faqs.question: How does the Kirin 820 handle gaming?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 820 handles mobile gaming well for the mid-range segment. The Mali-G57 MP6 GPU delivers smooth performance in popular titles: PUBG Mobile runs at HD graphics with 40fps; Call of Duty Mobile runs smoothly at high settings; Genshin Impact is playable at medium settings with 30fps. The processor supports GPU Turbo technology for optimized gaming performance. While it cannot match flagship processors for the most demanding games at maximum settings, it provides an excellent gaming experience for the target market. Thermal management is easier than flagships, allowing sustained performance during longer gaming sessions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Kirin 820 is suitable for mainstream gaming phones targeting casual to moderate gamers.

faqs.question: What AI features does Kirin 820 support?
faqs.answer: The Kirin 820's single-core NPU delivers 4 TOPS of AI performance, enabling a wide range of on-device AI features: 1) AI-enhanced photography including scene recognition, portrait mode, and night mode; 2) Real-time video enhancement and stabilization; 3) Voice recognition and noise cancellation; 4) Intelligent task scheduling for power optimization; 5) Face unlock and biometric authentication; 6) AI-powered app preloading for faster launch times. While less powerful than flagship NPUs, the 4 TOPS performance handles most common AI use cases effectively without cloud dependency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage the NPU for AI-enhanced camera features and intelligent system optimization.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select HiSilicon AI Accelerators
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Ascend AI chip for your application

faqs.question: What are the key features of Ascend AI accelerators?
faqs.answer: Ascend AI accelerators feature: 1) Dedicated AI cores optimized for neural network inference and training; 2) Industry-leading TOPS-per-watt performance for power-efficient AI processing; 3) Support for popular AI frameworks including TensorFlow, PyTorch, and MindSpore; 4) Scalable architecture from edge devices to data center servers; 5) Comprehensive software stack with compilers, optimizers, and profiling tools; 6) Hardware security features for AI model protection. These features make Ascend ideal for applications requiring real-time AI processing with strict power constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the specifications to identify the Ascend model that meets your AI performance and power requirements.

faqs.question: How do I choose between Ascend 310 and Ascend 910?
faqs.answer: Ascend 310 and 910 serve different application domains: Ascend 310 is designed for edge AI applications with 16 TOPS INT8 performance and 8W typical power consumption, ideal for cameras, robots, and edge gateways. Ascend 910 is a data center AI processor delivering 256-512 TFLOPS FP16 performance with 310W power consumption, designed for training large AI models. Choose Ascend 310 for inference at the edge where power and cost are critical, and Ascend 910 for data center training and high-throughput inference. Both share the same Da Vinci architecture and software stack for easy migration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your deployment location (edge vs. data center) and AI workload type (inference vs. training) to select the appropriate Ascend model.

faqs.question: What AI frameworks are supported by Ascend chips?
faqs.answer: Ascend AI accelerators support a comprehensive range of AI frameworks: 1) MindSpore - Huawei's native AI framework with optimal Ascend optimization; 2) TensorFlow and TensorFlow Lite - via graph conversion tools; 3) PyTorch - through ONNX export and conversion; 4) ONNX - direct support for standardized models; 5) Caffe and Caffe2 - via model conversion; 6) Custom operators - through CANN (Compute Architecture for Neural Networks) SDK. The CANN software stack provides model conversion, optimization, and deployment tools to run models from any framework efficiently on Ascend hardware.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MindSpore for best performance, or convert your existing TensorFlow/PyTorch models using the CANN conversion tools.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of Ascend AI accelerators?
faqs.answer: Ascend AI accelerator power consumption varies by model: Ascend 310 (edge) consumes 8W typical with 16 TOPS INT8 performance, achieving 2 TOPS/W efficiency. Ascend 310P (enhanced edge) consumes 15W with 22 TOPS performance. Ascend 910 (data center) consumes 310W with 256 TFLOPS FP16 performance, delivering excellent training efficiency. Ascend 910B (enhanced) reaches 376 TFLOPS at 310W. All models include dynamic power management and can operate in reduced power modes when full performance is not required.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Ascend 310 series for edge applications with strict power budgets, and Ascend 910 series for data center applications.

faqs.question: What development tools are available for Ascend platforms?
faqs.answer: HiSilicon provides comprehensive development tools for Ascend: 1) CANN (Compute Architecture for Neural Networks) - core software stack including drivers, runtime, and libraries; 2) MindStudio - integrated development environment for model development and optimization; 3) Model conversion tools - convert TensorFlow, PyTorch, and ONNX models to Ascend format; 4) Profiling tools - analyze model performance and identify bottlenecks; 5) SDK and APIs - for custom operator development; 6) Reference designs and sample applications. BeiLuo provides technical support and training for Ascend development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo to access Ascend development tools and receive training on the CANN software stack.

faqs.question: What is the maximum video processing capability of Ascend 310?
faqs.answer: The Ascend 310 supports impressive video processing capabilities: 16-channel 1080p H.264/H.265 video decoding at 30fps per channel, and 8-channel 1080p video encoding. This enables processing of multi-camera feeds simultaneously for applications like video surveillance, retail analytics, and traffic monitoring. The video processing engines work in parallel with the AI cores, allowing real-time inference on all video streams without frame drops. For higher resolutions, the Ascend 310 can handle 4-channel 4K video decoding or 2-channel 4K encoding.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your camera system within these limits, or contact us for solutions requiring higher channel counts.

faqs.question: How does Ascend 310 compare to GPU-based AI accelerators?
faqs.answer: Compared to GPU-based accelerators, Ascend 310 offers several advantages: 1) Higher TOPS-per-watt efficiency - 2 TOPS/W vs. typically 0.5-1 TOPS/W for GPUs; 2) Lower cost - optimized for inference without GPU overhead; 3) Better deterministic latency - designed for real-time applications; 4) Lower thermal requirements - passive cooling possible; 5) Purpose-built AI architecture - Da Vinci cores optimized for neural networks vs. general-purpose GPU. However, GPUs offer broader software ecosystem (CUDA) and may be preferable for development flexibility or applications requiring both AI and graphics processing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Ascend 310 for power-efficient, cost-optimized inference deployments; choose GPUs for development flexibility or graphics+AI workloads.

faqs.question: What is the typical latency for inference on Ascend 310?
faqs.answer: Ascend 310 delivers low-latency inference suitable for real-time applications: Single image classification (ResNet-50) typically completes in 2-4ms; Object detection (YOLOv3) processes in 10-20ms per frame; Face detection runs at sub-10ms per frame. These latencies enable real-time processing at 30-60fps for most computer vision applications. The deterministic architecture ensures consistent latency, critical for time-sensitive applications like autonomous systems. Actual latency depends on model complexity, input resolution, and batch size.
faqs.decisionGuide: Benchmark your specific model on the Ascend 310 development kit to verify latency meets your application requirements.

faqs.question: Does Ascend 310 support model quantization?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ascend 310 supports INT8 quantization for optimal performance. The CANN software stack provides automatic quantization tools that convert FP32 models to INT8 with minimal accuracy loss. Quantization typically achieves 2x performance improvement with less than 1% accuracy degradation for most computer vision models. The Ascend 310 also supports FP16 for applications requiring higher precision. Mixed precision is supported, allowing different layers to use different precision levels. The quantization-aware training feature in MindSpore can further improve quantized model accuracy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use INT8 quantization for production deployment to maximize performance; use FP16 if accuracy requirements demand higher precision.

faqs.question: What thermal solution is required for Ascend 310?
faqs.answer: The Ascend 310's 8W power consumption allows flexible thermal solutions: Passive cooling with a heatsink is sufficient for most applications with adequate airflow; Fanless designs are achievable in well-ventilated enclosures; Active cooling with a small fan enables higher ambient temperatures. The processor includes thermal monitoring and throttling protection. For industrial applications, ensure the junction temperature stays below 105°C. The compact thermal profile makes Ascend 310 suitable for space-constrained installations where larger GPUs won't fit.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your thermal solution based on ambient conditions and enclosure constraints. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling assistance.

faqs.question: What is the training throughput of Ascend 910 for popular models?
faqs.answer: Ascend 910 delivers impressive training throughput: ResNet-50 training achieves ~1800 images/second per card; BERT-Large pre-training processes ~100 sequences/second; GPT-3 style models train at competitive speeds with good scaling efficiency. The HBM2 memory bandwidth (1.2 TB/s) is crucial for large model training, eliminating the memory bottlenecks common with GDDR-based accelerators. When scaled to clusters, Ascend 910 maintains good linear scaling efficiency up to hundreds of cards with proper interconnect configuration. Actual throughput depends on model architecture, batch size, and data pipeline optimization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Benchmark your specific model on Ascend 910 to verify performance meets your training time requirements.

faqs.question: How does Ascend 910 compare to NVIDIA A100 for training?
faqs.answer: Ascend 910 and A100 are both high-performance AI training accelerators with different strengths: Ascend 910 offers 256 TFLOPS FP16 vs A100's 312 TFLOPS, making A100 about 22% faster in raw compute. However, Ascend 910 has better power efficiency (0.83 vs 0.78 TFLOPS/W) and lower power consumption (310W vs 400W). A100 has advantages in ecosystem maturity (CUDA vs CANN) and memory capacity (80GB vs 32GB). For large models that don't fit in 32GB, A100's larger memory is significant. For models that fit, Ascend 910 offers competitive performance with better TCO. Both support similar precision formats (FP16, BF16, INT8).
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Ascend 910 for cost-optimized training of models fitting in 32GB; choose A100 for maximum ecosystem compatibility or very large models.

faqs.question: What is the scaling efficiency for Ascend 910 clusters?
faqs.answer: Ascend 910 clusters achieve excellent scaling efficiency: 16-card clusters typically achieve 90-95% linear scaling; 128-card clusters maintain 85-90% efficiency; 1024-card clusters achieve 80-85% efficiency with proper configuration. The 100Gbps RoCE interconnect provides high-bandwidth, low-latency communication between cards. The CANN software stack includes optimized collective communication libraries (similar to NCCL) for efficient distributed training. For maximum scaling efficiency, use the recommended network topology (fat-tree or dragonfly) and ensure proper data pipeline optimization to keep GPUs/VPUs fed with data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your cluster with proper interconnect topology and work with our solutions team for large-scale deployment optimization.

faqs.question: Does Ascend 910 support inference as well as training?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ascend 910 excels at both training and high-throughput inference: For training, the 256 TFLOPS FP16 performance and HBM2 bandwidth accelerate large model training. For inference, the 512 TOPS INT8 performance enables extremely high throughput - up to 100,000+ images/second for ResNet-50. Many customers use Ascend 910 for both training and inference deployment, simplifying their AI infrastructure. The high memory bandwidth is particularly beneficial for large model inference (like GPT-3 style models) where weight loading can be a bottleneck. For pure inference deployments, consider Ascend 310 for edge or cost-optimized data center inference.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Ascend 910 for training+inference combined workloads or maximum inference throughput; use Ascend 310 for cost-optimized inference.

faqs.question: What is the typical power and cooling requirement for Ascend 910 servers?
faqs.answer: Ascend 910 servers require significant power and cooling infrastructure: Each Ascend 910 card consumes 310W, so an 8-card server requires ~2500W plus CPU and system power (total ~3000W). For a 42U rack with 8 servers, plan for 25-30kW power capacity. Cooling requirements depend on deployment density: Air-cooled servers need 150-200 CFM per card; Liquid cooling enables higher density with 40-50kW per rack. Data center ambient temperature should be maintained at 18-27°C for optimal performance. The power supplies are typically 80 PLUS Platinum or Titanium for efficiency. Work with our solutions team for data center design guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your data center can support the power and cooling requirements before deploying Ascend 910 clusters.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select HiSilicon Server Processors
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Kunpeng processor for your server deployment

faqs.question: What are the key advantages of Kunpeng ARM server processors?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng ARM server processors offer several key advantages: 1) Excellent performance-per-watt - ARM architecture delivers superior energy efficiency compared to x86; 2) Native ARM ecosystem - optimized for mobile and cloud-native applications already running on ARM; 3) High core density - up to 128 cores per socket for massive parallel processing; 4) Robust security features - hardware encryption, secure boot, and trusted execution; 5) Cost optimization - competitive pricing with lower TCO for scale-out workloads; 6) Scalability - from single-socket edge servers to 8-socket data center platforms. These advantages make Kunpeng ideal for cloud infrastructure, containerized workloads, and ARM-native applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate Kunpeng for cloud, big data, and scale-out workloads where energy efficiency and core density are important.

faqs.question: How does Kunpeng compare to Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng competes with x86 servers in specific workloads: Performance-per-watt - Kunpeng typically offers 20-30% better efficiency; Core density - Kunpeng 920 has 64 cores vs. Xeon's typical 32-40 cores; Memory bandwidth - competitive with DDR4-2933 support; Single-thread performance - x86 generally leads for legacy applications; Software compatibility - x86 has broader legacy support, but Kunpeng excels with ARM-native and open-source software. For cloud-native applications, containers, and microservices, Kunpeng performance is competitive. For legacy enterprise applications requiring x86, migration may require recompilation or emulation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Kunpeng for cloud-native, containerized, and ARM-optimized workloads; choose x86 for legacy applications requiring maximum single-thread performance.

faqs.question: What software runs on Kunpeng servers?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng servers support a comprehensive software ecosystem: Operating systems - CentOS, Ubuntu, openEuler (optimized for Kunpeng), and Kylin OS; Cloud platforms - OpenStack, Kubernetes, and Docker; Databases - MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and Redis (all ARM-optimized); Big data - Hadoop, Spark, and Flink; Web servers - Nginx, Apache, and Tomcat; Programming languages - Java, Python, Go, and Node.js with native ARM support. Most open-source software has native ARM support. Proprietary software may require ARM versions or emulation. Huawei provides migration tools and compatibility testing services.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your software stack compatibility with Kunpeng. Most open-source and cloud-native software runs natively.

faqs.question: What is the typical power consumption of Kunpeng processors?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng processor power consumption varies by model: Kunpeng 916 (32-core) has 85W TDP for entry-level servers; Kunpeng 920 (48-core) has 150W TDP for mainstream deployment; Kunpeng 920 (64-core) has 180W TDP for high-performance servers. The power efficiency is typically 20-30% better than comparable x86 processors. For a dual-socket server, expect 300-400W for CPUs plus system power. The excellent performance-per-watt reduces data center cooling costs and enables higher density deployments. Power management features include dynamic frequency scaling and core parking for idle power reduction.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Kunpeng models based on your performance requirements and power budget. All models offer excellent efficiency.

faqs.question: What server platforms are available for Kunpeng processors?
faqs.answer: Multiple server platforms support Kunpeng processors: Huawei FusionServer - comprehensive range from 1U to 8U form factors; TaiShan servers - optimized for Kunpeng with balanced performance and cost; Third-party servers - Inspur, H3C, and other vendors offer Kunpeng-based systems; Edge servers - compact designs for edge computing deployments; Custom designs - ODM partners can develop custom Kunpeng servers. Form factors include rackmount (1U/2U/4U), blade servers, and high-density multi-node systems. BeiLuo can help select the optimal platform for your deployment requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact BeiLuo to discuss your server requirements and select the optimal Kunpeng platform.

products.description: The Kunpeng 920 is a high-performance 64-core ARM server processor designed for data center and cloud computing applications. With 8-channel DDR4 memory support and excellent power efficiency, it delivers exceptional performance for scale-out workloads.
products.longDescription: The Kunpeng 920 represents HiSilicon's flagship server processor, bringing ARM architecture advantages to the data center. With 64 cores and high memory bandwidth, it excels at parallel workloads.
descriptionParagraphs: The Kunpeng 920 features 64 ARMv8.2 cores running at 2.6GHz, delivering massive parallel processing capability for cloud and big data applications.
With 8-channel DDR4-2933 memory support providing 192GB/s bandwidth, the Kunpeng 920 eliminates memory bottlenecks for data-intensive workloads.
The 180W TDP and advanced power management deliver excellent performance-per-watt, reducing data center operating costs.

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Computing
faeReview.content: The Kunpeng 920 is a compelling alternative to x86 for cloud and scale-out workloads. In my deployments, the 64-core configuration handles containerized microservices exceptionally well - much better than x86 at similar price points. The memory bandwidth from 8 channels is a standout feature; big data workloads like Spark see significant performance gains. I've successfully migrated several customers from x86 to Kunpeng for their cloud infrastructure with 20-30% cost savings. The key is ensuring your software stack is ARM-compatible - most modern open-source software is, but verify proprietary applications. For new cloud-native deployments, I strongly recommend evaluating Kunpeng.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent core density and memory bandwidth for cloud and big data workloads

alternativeParts.reason: Market-leading x86 with mature ecosystem but higher power and fewer cores
Lower core count variant for cost-optimized deployments
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications not requiring maximum core density

faqs.question: What is the performance of Kunpeng 920 for cloud workloads?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng 920 delivers excellent performance for cloud-native workloads: Container density - up to 4x more containers than comparable x86 due to higher core count; Microservices - low latency and high throughput for service mesh applications; Kubernetes - efficient orchestration with native ARM support; Serverless - fast cold start times for function-as-a-service. In benchmarks, Kunpeng 920 shows 20-50% better performance-per-watt than x86 for typical cloud workloads. The 8-channel memory is crucial for memory-intensive applications like in-memory databases and caching layers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Deploy Kunpeng 920 for cloud infrastructure to maximize container density and energy efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I migrate my applications to Kunpeng?
faqs.answer: Migrating to Kunpeng involves several steps: 1) Compatibility assessment - identify ARM-native vs. x86-only software; 2) Recompilation - most C/C++ applications compile cleanly for ARM; 3) Java/Python/Go apps - typically run without changes on ARM; 4) Database migration - MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB all have ARM versions; 5) Testing - validate performance and functionality; 6) Deployment - use containers for consistent environments. Huawei provides migration tools including binary analysis, dependency checking, and automated testing. BeiLuo offers migration consulting services to ensure smooth transitions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with a pilot migration of non-critical workloads to validate your software stack on Kunpeng.

faqs.question: What virtualization support does Kunpeng 920 provide?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng 920 provides comprehensive virtualization support: Hardware virtualization extensions in the ARMv8.2 architecture; KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) for full virtualization; Docker and container runtimes for OS-level virtualization; Hardware I/O virtualization (SR-IOV) for network and storage; Nested virtualization support for cloud environments. The 64 cores enable high VM density - typically 2-4x more VMs per server than x86 alternatives. Popular virtualization platforms including OpenStack and VMware ESXi-ARM are supported.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Kunpeng 920 for virtualized infrastructure to maximize VM density and reduce server sprawl.

faqs.question: What is the memory capacity and bandwidth of Kunpeng 920?
faqs.answer: Kunpeng 920 supports impressive memory configurations: Capacity - up to 4TB per socket (8 channels x 512GB); Bandwidth - 192 GB/s theoretical peak (8 channels x DDR4-2933); RDIMM and LRDIMM support for different capacity needs; ECC support for data integrity; Memory interleaving for optimal bandwidth utilization. The high memory bandwidth is particularly beneficial for: In-memory databases (Redis, Memcached); Big data processing (Spark, Hadoop); Large-scale virtualization; High-performance computing. The 8-channel design provides 33% more bandwidth than typical 6-channel x86 processors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Leverage the high memory bandwidth for data-intensive applications and large in-memory workloads.

faqs.question: Does Kunpeng 920 support dual-socket configurations?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kunpeng 920 supports dual-socket configurations for maximum performance: Up to 128 cores and 8TB memory in a 2-socket server; Cache coherency between sockets via high-speed interconnect; NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) architecture for optimal memory access; Balanced performance for scale-up workloads. Dual-socket Kunpeng servers are ideal for: Large database servers; High-density virtualization; Memory-intensive applications; Scale-up enterprise workloads. Single-socket configurations offer cost savings for less demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose dual-socket for maximum performance and memory capacity; single-socket for cost-optimized deployments.

It offers about 75% of the 64-core performance at significantly lower cost, making it ideal for mainstream applications that don't need maximum core density. I've deployed these in web server farms, application servers, and development environments with excellent results. The 150W TDP is easy to cool, and the performance-per-dollar is very competitive. The software compatibility is identical to the 64-core version, so no concerns there.
faeReview.highlight: Cost-optimized ARM server processor with excellent mainstream performance

alternativeParts.reason: x86 alternative with mature ecosystem but fewer cores and higher power
Higher core count variant for maximum parallel processing

faqs.question: What is the performance difference between 48-core and 64-core Kunpeng 920?
faqs.answer: The 48-core Kunpeng 920 delivers approximately 75% of the 64-core performance in multi-threaded workloads. Single-thread performance is identical since both run at 2.6GHz. The main differences: Multi-threaded throughput - 48-core achieves ~75% of 64-core; Memory bandwidth - identical 8-channel configuration; Power consumption - 150W vs 180W (-17%); Cost - significantly lower price per processor. For applications that don't fully utilize 64 cores, the 48-core offers better value. Both support the same software ecosystem and server platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose 48-core for cost-optimized deployments; choose 64-core for maximum parallel processing requirements.

faqs.question: Is the 48-core Kunpeng suitable for virtualization?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 48-core Kunpeng 920 is excellent for virtualization: VM density - supports 50-100 VMs per server depending on workload; Container density - 200+ containers for microservices deployments; Hypervisor support - KVM, Docker, and Kubernetes all run natively; Hardware virtualization - full support for nested virtualization and SR-IOV. The 48 cores provide ample capacity for medium-sized virtualization deployments. For larger environments, consider dual-socket configurations or the 64-core variant. The power efficiency reduces operating costs for virtualized infrastructure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Deploy 48-core Kunpeng for medium-scale virtualization with excellent cost-efficiency.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for 48-core Kunpeng servers?
faqs.answer: The 48-core Kunpeng 920's 150W TDP enables flexible cooling options: Air cooling - standard server heatsinks with 30-40 CFM airflow; Rack cooling - standard data center cooling adequate for most deployments; Liquid cooling - available for high-density deployments but not required; Fanless - not recommended due to 150W heat output. The lower power compared to x86 alternatives (typically 200W+) simplifies cooling design and reduces data center cooling costs. Ensure adequate airflow (front-to-back) in rack deployments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard air cooling is sufficient for most 48-core Kunpeng deployments.

faqs.question: Does the 48-core support the same memory as the 64-core?
faqs.answer: Yes, both 48-core and 64-core Kunpeng 920 support identical memory configurations: 8-channel DDR4-2933 for maximum bandwidth; Up to 4TB capacity per socket; RDIMM and LRDIMM support; ECC for data integrity; Same memory controller architecture. The memory performance is identical between variants. This means you can standardize on memory configurations across your Kunpeng server fleet regardless of core count. The 8-channel design provides 33% more bandwidth than typical 6-channel x86 processors, benefiting memory-intensive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use the same memory configuration strategy for both 48-core and 64-core deployments.

faqs.question: What are typical use cases for 48-core Kunpeng?
faqs.answer: The 48-core Kunpeng 920 excels in several use cases: Web servers - high concurrent connection handling with excellent power efficiency; Application servers - Java, Python, and Node.js applications run efficiently; Development environments - cost-effective build and test servers; Edge computing - sufficient performance with lower power for edge locations; Caching layers - Redis, Memcached with high memory bandwidth; Small-scale databases - MySQL, PostgreSQL for moderate workloads. The 48-core is not ideal for: Large-scale databases requiring maximum cores; High-density virtualization (choose 64-core); HPC applications needing maximum parallel throughput.
faqs.decisionGuide: Deploy 48-core Kunpeng for mainstream server workloads where cost-efficiency is important.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select HiSilicon Connectivity Solutions
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right Balong modem for your connectivity requirements

faqs.question: What cellular technologies do Balong modems support?
faqs.answer: Balong modems support comprehensive cellular technologies: 5G NR - both SA (Standalone) and NSA (Non-Standalone) modes with sub-6GHz and mmWave support; LTE - Cat 4 through Cat 20 with carrier aggregation; 3G/HSPA+ - for legacy network compatibility; 2G/GSM - for global coverage and fallback; NB-IoT and eMTC - for low-power IoT applications. The multi-mode architecture enables seamless handover between technologies for optimal connectivity. All Balong modems support global frequency bands for worldwide deployment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select Balong modems based on your target network technology and regional band requirements.

faqs.question: How do I choose between integrated and discrete Balong modems?
faqs.answer: Integrated modems (in Kirin processors) offer advantages: Lower power consumption - shared power management and no inter-chip communication; Smaller PCB footprint - no separate modem chip; Cost optimization - single chip vs. two chips; Better thermal management - shared cooling solution. Discrete modems (Balong 5000, 711) offer: Flexibility - pair with any application processor; Upgrade path - swap modem without changing AP; Multi-AP support - one modem for multiple processors. Choose integrated for smartphones and tablets where space and power are critical. Choose discrete for IoT devices, industrial systems, and applications requiring modem flexibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use integrated modems for mobile devices; use discrete modems for IoT and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What are the data rates of Balong 5G modems?
faqs.answer: Balong 5G modems deliver industry-leading data rates: Balong 5000 - up to 6.5 Gbps download and 3.5 Gbps upload (sub-6GHz); Balong 711 - up to 2.5 Gbps download for IoT applications; LTE fallback - up to 2 Gbps with Cat 20. Real-world speeds depend on network conditions, carrier configuration, and signal strength. Typical user experience: 100-500 Mbps download in good 5G coverage; 20-100 Mbps in LTE mode; Latency as low as 1-4ms in 5G SA mode. The modems support carrier aggregation for enhanced speeds and seamless mobility between cells.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balong modems provide more than adequate bandwidth for any mobile or IoT application.

faqs.question: What carrier certifications do Balong modems have?
faqs.answer: Balong modems hold comprehensive carrier certifications: Global - GCF (Global Certification Forum) for worldwide compatibility; North America - FCC, PTCRB, and major carrier certifications (Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile); Europe - CE marking and carrier certifications; Asia - CCC (China), TELEC (Japan), KC (Korea); Regional - certifications for Middle East, Latin America, Africa. The certification portfolio enables deployment on virtually any carrier network worldwide. For specific carrier requirements, contact BeiLuo to verify current certification status.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balong modems are certified for global deployment on major carrier networks.

faqs.question: What power consumption can I expect from Balong modems?
faqs.answer: Balong modem power consumption varies by mode: 5G active - 500-800mW during data transfer; LTE active - 300-500mW typical; Idle/connected - 10-30mW in power save mode; Sleep - <1mW for IoT applications. The advanced power management includes: Adaptive power control based on signal strength; Discontinuous reception (DRX) for idle power savings; Carrier aggregation power optimization; Temperature-based power limiting. For battery-powered devices, the low power consumption extends battery life significantly compared to older generation modems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balong modems offer excellent power efficiency for both mobile and battery-powered IoT applications.

faqs.question: What is the real-world 5G speed of Balong 5000?
faqs.answer: Real-world 5G speeds with Balong 5000 depend on network conditions: Optimal conditions - 1-2 Gbps download near cell towers; Good coverage - 200-500 Mbps typical in urban areas; Indoor/edge - 50-100 Mbps at cell edges; Upload speeds - typically 20-100 Mbps. The 6.5 Gbps peak is achieved only in ideal lab conditions with maximum carrier aggregation. Latency in 5G SA mode is typically 10-20ms, significantly better than LTE's 30-50ms. Network congestion, signal strength, and carrier configuration all affect actual speeds. The modem supports carrier aggregation to maximize available bandwidth.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 200-500 Mbps typical speeds in good 5G coverage - more than adequate for any mobile application.

faqs.question: Does Balong 5000 support mmWave 5G?
faqs.answer: The Balong 5000 primarily supports sub-6GHz 5G bands (n1, n3, n28, n41, n77, n78, n79) which provide the best coverage and building penetration. Sub-6GHz is the dominant 5G technology globally, used by carriers in Europe, Asia, and most of North America. mmWave support (n257, n260, n261) is available in select variants for markets like the US where mmWave is deployed. mmWave offers higher speeds but limited range and poor indoor coverage. For most applications, sub-6GHz provides the optimal balance of speed and coverage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Sub-6GHz Balong 5000 is suitable for global deployment; select mmWave variants only if specifically required.

faqs.question: How does the integrated vs. discrete Balong 5000 compare?
faqs.answer: Integrated (in Kirin 9000) and discrete Balong 5000 offer the same core 5G capabilities but different integration options: Integrated advantages - lower power (shared power management), smaller PCB area, lower BOM cost, better thermal management. Discrete advantages - works with any application processor, upgrade path without changing AP, flexible placement on PCB. Performance is identical - same modem IP, same RF capabilities, same data rates. Power consumption is 20-30% lower in integrated configuration due to optimized power management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use integrated for smartphones and tablets; use discrete for IoT, industrial, and multi-AP applications.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption during different 5G operations?
faqs.answer: Balong 5000 power consumption varies by operation: High-speed download - 700-800mW during 1+ Gbps transfers; Typical browsing - 400-500mW for web and social media; Video streaming - 500-600mW for HD video; Voice over 5G - 200-300mW; Idle connected - 20-30mW in power save mode; Sleep - <1mW. The integrated version in Kiron 9000 achieves 20-30% lower power due to shared power management. Advanced power saving features include adaptive power control based on throughput needs and intelligent sleep modes during inactivity.
faqs.decisionGuide: The Balong 5000 power consumption is competitive with other 5G modems and enables good battery life.

faqs.question: What carriers and regions is Balong 5000 certified for?
faqs.answer: Balong 5000 has extensive carrier certifications: North America - FCC, PTCRB, Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile; Europe - CE marking, major carriers (Vodafone, Deutsche Telekom, Orange); Asia - CCC (China), TELEC (Japan), KC (Korea), NCC (Taiwan); Global - GCF certification for worldwide compatibility. The modem supports all major 5G bands used globally, enabling a single SKU for worldwide deployment. For specific carrier certification status and band support, contact BeiLuo to verify current certifications as they are regularly updated.
faqs.decisionGuide: Balong 5000 is ready for global deployment on major carrier networks.

faqs.question: What makes Balong 711 suitable for IoT vs. smartphone modems?
faqs.answer: Balong 711 is optimized for IoT in several ways: Lower power - 30% reduction in active power consumption extends battery life; Industrial temperature - -40 to +85°C range for harsh environments; Simplified features - removes smartphone-specific features not needed for IoT; Cost optimization - reduced BOM cost for price-sensitive IoT; Compact size - smaller footprint for space-constrained devices; NB-IoT support - includes low-power wide-area connectivity. Smartphone modems are optimized for maximum speed and features, often overkill for IoT. The 711 provides the right balance of 5G capability, power efficiency, and cost for connected devices.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Balong 711 for IoT applications; use smartphone modems only if maximum 5G speed is required.

faqs.question: What battery life can I expect with Balong 711?
faqs.answer: Battery life with Balong 711 depends on usage patterns: Always-connected gateway - 2-5 years on large battery (10Ah+); Periodic reporting sensor - 5-10 years on AA batteries; High-bandwidth application - days to weeks depending on data volume. The modem's power saving features help maximize battery life: eDRX (extended Discontinuous Reception) for long sleep cycles; PSM (Power Saving Mode) for ultra-low power standby; Adaptive power based on signal strength. For maximum battery life, use NB-IoT mode when high bandwidth isn't needed - power consumption drops to microamps in sleep.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your power budget using the 300-500mW active and <1mW sleep figures for your specific duty cycle.

faqs.question: Does Balong 711 support NB-IoT and LTE-M?
faqs.answer: Yes, Balong 711 supports comprehensive IoT connectivity: NB-IoT (Narrowband IoT) - ultra-low power, deep coverage, up to 200kbps; LTE-M (eMTC) - medium data rates, mobility support, up to 1Mbps; LTE Cat 1 - higher data rates for multimedia IoT; 5G NR - high bandwidth when needed. This multi-mode support allows devices to select the optimal technology based on coverage, power budget, and data requirements. NB-IoT is ideal for stationary sensors with infrequent transmissions. LTE-M supports mobile applications like asset tracking. 5G provides high bandwidth for firmware updates or video applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NB-IoT for maximum battery life; LTE-M for mobile IoT; 5G for high-bandwidth applications.

faqs.question: What form factors are available for Balong 711?
faqs.answer: Balong 711 is available in multiple form factors: Discrete chip - BGA package for custom PCB designs; M.2 module - 3042 or 3052 form factor for easy integration; LGA module - land grid array for industrial applications; PCIe card - half-mini or full-size for gateway applications. The M.2 form factor is most popular for rapid prototyping and low-volume production. The discrete chip offers lowest cost and smallest size for high-volume products. All form factors provide the same RF and protocol capabilities.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use M.2 for prototyping and low volume; use discrete chip for high-volume cost optimization.
